      681 +| **Last updated** | 2026-05-16 |                                                                        
      682 +| **Research** | `docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md` (verification-as-first-class-phase moat); TRON 
          +evaluation 2026-05-16; `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §5 |                                                       
      683 +| **Source repo** | TRON: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Utilities/tron` (v5.4, "Implementation Ready") | 
      684  
      681 -*Placeholder.* Full PRD to be authored after Phase 1 (TRON subtree merge) is complete and we have hands-o
          -n experience with the integration boundaries. See `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-8 epics (`EPIC-8.1` through `EPI
          -C-8.6`) and `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §5 for the TRON → Spine code mapping that is the v1 scope.            
      685 +### 8.1. Summary                                                                                         
      686  
      687 +Integrate **TRON** — Python+FastAPI+Temporal enterprise AI QA platform with 7-layer verification + ISO-ag
          +ent swarm — into Spine as the **canonical Verify subsystem**. Integration via `git subtree` into `verify/
          +` (preserves TRON's git history + internal cohesion). Once integrated, TRON's `AuditManager` is invoked b
          +y Orchestrator at Verify phase via MCP (`verify_audit(build_artifact, blueprint) → VerifyFindings`). Cros
          +s-cutting TRON capabilities (Standards Hierarchy, MCP, memory, parsers, frontend, infra) move to `shared/
          +`. **TRON must continue to run standalone** after integration.                                           
      688 +                                                                                                         
      689 +Closes Spine's biggest functional gap (no verification subsystem) by lifting a shipped product into the u
          +mbrella — no rewrite, no loss of standalone positioning.                                                 
      690 +                                                                                                         
      691 +### 8.2. Problem                                                                                         
      692 +                                                                                                         
      693 +Spine today has no verification subsystem. Build emits code + self-report; nothing independently confirms
          + correctness, safety, scope, or execution. Auditing is a role prompt, not a pipeline; no sandbox, no cali
          +bration, no cross-LLM consensus, no deterministic scanner baseline. The "verification-as-first-class-phas
          +e" sixth corner of the moat is aspirational.                                                             
      694 +                                                                                                         
      695 +Meanwhile **TRON exists and is built well** — 7-layer pipeline shipping, ISO agent swarm under `AuditMana
          +ger`, Docker sandbox with seccomp, Platt-scaled calibration, cross-LLM validation, prompt-regression CI, 
          +golden test suite, audit webhooks, MinIO SARIF archival, scan handoff exports. Re-implementing inside Spi
          +ne would waste effort *and* split verification into two divergent codebases.                             
      696 +                                                                                                         
      697 +**Spine needs verification; TRON is verification.** Question is integration shape: how to make TRON avail
          +able to Orchestrator without forking, breaking standalone deployment, or freezing TRON's evolution.      
      698 +                                                                                                         
      699 +### 8.3. Users & stakeholders                                                                            
      700 +                                                                                                         
      701 +| Stakeholder | What they need |                                                                         
      702 +|---|---|                                                                                                
      703 +| **Spine Orchestrator** | Single MCP tool (`verify_audit`); routes Verify failure back to Build |       
      704 +| **Spine Build** | Optional early-detect — call individual ISO agents from inside Build before declaring
          + done |                                                                                                  
      705 +| **TRON audit-only users** | TRON keeps working standalone; no breaking changes |                       
      706 +| **Vibecoder (end user)** | Enterprise-grade verification with explicit provenance, calibrated confidenc
          +e, sandbox-confirmed fixes |                                                                             
      707 +| **Org admin** | Configure which ISO agents run via org bundle; require ComplianceISO for regulated work
          +loads |                                                                                                  
      708 +| **Auditor / Security** | Audit trail of every Verify run in unified `spine_audit`; per-finding provenan
          +ce |                                                                                                     
      709 +| **TRON maintainers** | Internal cohesion preserved — tests, CI, Docker stack, conventions intact under 
          +`verify/` |                                                                                              
      710 +                                                                                                         
      711 +### 8.4. Goals                                                                                           
      712 +                                                                                                         
      713 +#### MUST (P0)                                                                                           
      714 +                                                                                                         
      715 +- **G-1** TRON integrated under `verify/` via `git subtree add` (preserves history).                     
      716 +- **G-2** TRON's test suite **passes from new location** with no regressions.                            
      717 +- **G-3** Orchestrator invokes Verify via `verify_audit(build_artifact, blueprint) → VerifyFindings`.    
      718 +- **G-4** Findings persist to `spine_audit` + `spine_verify_*`; surfaced through unified audit log.      
      719 +- **G-5** TRON Standards Hierarchy → `shared/standards/` (per `EPIC-2.4`).                               
      720 +- **G-6** TRON MCP server consolidates into `shared/mcp/` (per `EPIC-2.2`).                              
      721 +- **G-7** TRON memory → `shared/memory/`; coexists with Spine role-memory.                               
      722 +- **G-8** TRON **runs standalone** after integration — no required Orchestrator dependency.              
      723 +- **G-9** Verification is canonical SDLC phase in `sdlc-pipeline.yaml` between `build` and `acceptance`. 
      724 +                                                                                                         
      725 +#### SHOULD (P1)                                                                                         
      726 +                                                                                                         
      727 +- **G-10** ISO agents individually addressable from Build for early-detect.                              
      728 +- **G-11** TRON tree-sitter parsers → `build/kg/parsers/` (feed KG per `EPIC-8.2.4`).                    
      729 +- **G-12** TRON frontend → `shared/ui/`; `admin-ui/` retires.                                            
      730 +- **G-13** TRON infra (Vault, secrets) → `shared/infra/`.                                                
      731 +- **G-14** Verify-fail auto-generates remediation directive (delegates to `EPIC-9.8`).                   
      732 +- **G-15** Postgres migrations converge on Flyway; Alembic ports to Flyway SQL.                          
      733 +                                                                                                         
      734 +#### COULD (P2)                                                                                          
      735 +                                                                                                         
      736 +- **G-16** Org bundle override of which ISO agents run.                                                  
      737 +- **G-17** Pre-verify cost projection at phase start.                                                    
      738 +- **G-18** TRON `agent_handoff` promoted to `shared/handoff/` for Plan + Build.                          
      739 +                                                                                                         
      740 +#### WON'T                                                                                               
      741 +                                                                                                         
      742 +- Rewriting TRON in another language — Python+FastAPI+Temporal stays.                                    
      743 +- Forking TRON or breaking standalone deployment.                                                        
      744 +- Building a second verification pipeline.                                                               
      745 +- Replacing TRON's Temporal with Spine's bash daemons.                                                   
      746 +- Building new ISO agents in this REQ.                                                                   
      747 +                                                                                                         
      748 +### 8.5. Functional requirements                                                                         
      749 +                                                                                                         
      750 +#### FR-1 — Subtree migration                                                                            
      751 +                                                                                                         
      752 +`git subtree add --prefix=verify/ <tron-repo> main` — full history preserved. Single atomic operation; no
          + incremental file-by-file copy. Post-merge, internal absolute paths updated to relative where needed. Doc
          +ker compose paths adjusted. TRON pytest suite passes from `verify/` with zero test logic modifications.  
      753 +                                                                                                         
      754 +#### FR-2 — TRON → Spine code mapping                                                                    
      755 +                                                                                                         
      756 +Authoritative table in `ARCHITECTURE.md §5`. Summary: `tron/agents/` → `verify/agents/`; `tron/verificati
          +on/` → `verify/pipeline/`; `tron/sandbox/` → `verify/sandbox/`; `tron/workflows/` → `verify/workflows/`; 
          +`tron/api/` → `verify/api/`; `tron/standards/` → `shared/standards/`; `tron/mcp/` → `shared/mcp/`; `tron/
          +memory/` → `shared/memory/`; `tron/parsers/` → `build/kg/parsers/`; `tron/infra/` → `shared/infra/`; `tro
          +n/realtime/` → `shared/realtime/`; `frontend/` → `shared/ui/`; `admin-ui/` retires; `alembic/` → `shared/
          +db/alembic/` then migrates to Flyway.                                                                    
      757 +                                                                                                         
      758 +#### FR-3 — Postgres consolidation (Alembic → Flyway)                                                    
      759 +                                                                                                         
      760 +Single Postgres, multiple schemas: `spine_recording`, `spine_kg`, `spine_lifecycle`, `spine_audit`, `spin
          +e_verify_*`. Alembic migrations ported to Flyway SQL. Cutover in Phase 2; Phase 1 leaves TRON on Alembic 
          +to avoid coupling. Migration ordering preserved; data not lost. `db/` moves to `shared/db/`.             
      761 +                                                                                                         
      762 +#### FR-4 — Orchestrator wiring (`verify_audit` MCP tool)                                                
      763 +                                                                                                         
      764 +Single tool: `verify_audit(build_artifact: BuildArtifact, blueprint: Blueprint) → VerifyFindings`. `Build
          +Artifact` per `EPIC-7.4`. `Blueprint` shapes scope (file patterns, check types, ISO agent selection, NOT_
          +IN_SCOPE). `VerifyFindings` is typed envelope: list of `FindingOutput`, per-layer provenance, sandbox con
          +firmation, calibration band, overall pass/fail. Implementation delegates to TRON's `AuditManager` — no pa
          +rallel impl. Orchestrator writes findings to `spine_audit` and decides route-back vs surface based on sev
          +erity + locked pipeline gate config.                                                                     
      765 +                                                                                                         
      766 +#### FR-5 — ISO agents callable from Build (early-detect)                                                
      767 +                                                                                                         
      768 +Each ISO agent individually addressable via MCP: `iso_invoke(agent_name, code_region, blueprint) → Findin
          +gOutput[]`. Engineer can optionally invoke SecurityISO before completing security-sensitive directive. Pr
          +e-verify findings surface in Build report. Pre-verify cost counted against project budget (no double-char
          +ging via audit marker).                                                                                  
      769 +                                                                                                         
      770 +#### FR-6 — Verification as canonical SDLC phase                                                         
      771 +                                                                                                         
      772 +`sdlc-pipeline.yaml` gains `verify` phase between `build` and `acceptance`. Default invokes TRON 7-layer 
          +pipeline. Org bundles override which ISO agents run. Verify phase has its own gate (default: user approva
          +l of findings summary; configurable to auto-pass if zero high/critical). Failure routes back to Build wit
          +h auto-generated remediation directive (per `EPIC-9.8`); max-retry per `STORY-9.8.2`.                    
      773 +                                                                                                         
      774 +#### FR-7 — Standalone deployability preserved                                                           
      775 +                                                                                                         
      776 +TRON's `docker-compose.yml` continues to run standalone — Vault, MinIO, Temporal, Postgres, API, frontend
          + all up. Orchestrator's MCP tool calls TRON via the same FastAPI surface TRON exposes — no internal-only 
          +API. No required env vars, schemas, or services from outside `verify/` to run TRON audit-only. Phase 2 co
          +nsolidation is **additive** — TRON's standalone compose still wires its own services if umbrella isn't pr
          +esent.                                                                                                   
      777 +                                                                                                         
      778 +### 8.6. Non-functional requirements                                                                     
      779 +                                                                                                         
      780 +- **NFR-1 Performance:** `verify_audit` end-to-end (100-LOC change, default ISO swarm, no cross-LLM) ≤ 90
          +s p95. With cross-LLM ≤ 180s p95. Sandbox sub-step ≤ 30s p95. Subtree merge ≤ 5min wall-clock.           
      781 +- **NFR-2 Reliability:** Temporal durability preserved — verify runs survive worker restarts. Findings du
          +rable in Postgres before orchestrator notification. ISO agent failure doesn't block others; `AuditManager
          +` marks missing as `unrun`.                                                                              
      782 +- **NFR-3 Security:** Sandbox hardening preserved (Docker caps, read-only rootfs, network isolation, cust
          +om seccomp). Seccomp configurable per org bundle. Containers ephemeral. Sandbox can be disabled (degraded
          + mode logged).                                                                                           
      783 +- **NFR-4 Cost:** Default verify ≤ $1. Cross-LLM ≈ 2× cost; surfaced in cost meter. Sandbox compute (CPU-
          +sec, mem-sec) in unified ledger. No infra cost increase — single Postgres/Vault/MinIO/Temporal.          
      784 +- **NFR-5 Customizability:** Which ISO agents run is config; declared in `sdlc-pipeline.yaml`, override-a
          +ble by org bundle. Blueprints first-class.                                                               
      785 +                                                                                                         
      786 +### 8.7. Dependencies                                                                                    
      787 +                                                                                                         
      788 +- **TRON existing stack** — Python 3.11+, FastAPI, Temporal, Postgres (pgvector, ltree, pgcrypto), Redis,
          + MinIO, Vault, Docker. All preserved.                                                                    
      789 +- **`EPIC-2.4`** — Standards Hierarchy lift (joint ownership).                                           
      790 +- **`EPIC-2.2`** — `verify_audit` and `iso_invoke` live on shared MCP server.                            
      791 +- **`INIT-3 EPIC-3.5/6/7`** — Sandbox / Calibration / Cross-LLM lifts overlap.                           
      792 +- **`INIT-9`** — must exist before `verify_audit` has a caller.                                          
      793 +- **`INIT-7 EPIC-7.4`** — `BuildArtifact` is the input contract.                                         
      794 +                                                                                                         
      795 +### 8.8. Open questions                                                                                  
      796 +                                                                                                         
      797 +- **OQ-1:** Alembic vs Flyway convergence timing — Phase 2 (after subtree). Confirm.                     
      798 +- **OQ-2:** Compose consolidation — root-level for prod, per-subsystem for dev. Phase 2 decision.        
      799 +- **OQ-3:** TRON's Vault/MinIO/Temporal — TRON-specific or promote to umbrella? Recommend keep TRON-speci
          +fic in Phase 1; promote in Phase 2 only if needed.                                                       
      800 +- **OQ-4:** TRON CLI — `tron audit` becomes `spine verify audit`? Recommend umbrella dispatches but `tron
          +` CLI continues to work standalone.                                                                      
      801 +- **OQ-5:** `agent_handoff` promotion to `shared/handoff/` — yes, but separate REQ.                      
      802 +- **OQ-6:** Schema co-tenancy — start under `verify/schemas/`; promote case-by-case.                     
      803 +- **OQ-7:** Standalone TRON MCP after consolidation — package `shared/mcp/` to run from `verify/` alone. 
      804 +- **OQ-8:** Frontend merge timing — Phase 4.                                                             
      805 +                                                                                                         
      806 +### 8.9. Acceptance criteria                                                                             
      807 +                                                                                                         
      808 +**Integration mechanics:**                                                                               
      809 +- [ ] `git subtree add` completes; `git log -- verify/` shows TRON history preserved.                    
      810 +- [ ] TRON pytest suite passes from `verify/tests/` with zero modifications.                             
      811 +- [ ] TRON's `docker-compose.yml` brings full stack from `verify/docker-compose.yml`; `curl localhost:130
          +00/health` returns 200.                                                                                  
      812 +- [ ] `make verify-test` dispatches to TRON's internal test target.                                      
      813 +                                                                                                         
      814 +**Standalone deployability (hard requirement, G-8):**                                                    
      815 +- [ ] `cd verify/ && docker compose up -d` runs TRON audit-only with no Orchestrator / `plan/` / `build/`
          +.                                                                                                        
      816 +- [ ] Standalone audit completes end-to-end post-integration; findings persist; webhook fires; admin UI r
          +enders.                                                                                                  
      817 +- [ ] No env var / schema / service from outside `verify/` required.                                     
      818 +- [ ] TRON's `tron` CLI continues standalone.                                                            
      819 +                                                                                                         
      820 +**Orchestrator wiring:**                                                                                 
      821 +- [ ] `verify_audit` MCP tool callable from orchestrator; returns structured response.                   
      822 +- [ ] Orchestrator invokes Verify against Build output end-to-end (Sprint 2 happy path).                 
      823 +- [ ] Verify failure routes back to Build with remediation directive.                                    
      824 +- [ ] Findings in `spine_audit` with full provenance.                                                    
      825 +                                                                                                         
      826 +**Cross-cutting moves:**                                                                                 
      827 +- [ ] `tron/standards/` at `shared/standards/`; at least one Spine role reads from it.                   
      828 +- [ ] `tron/mcp/` consolidated; single MCP server registers Verify + Orchestrator tools.                 
      829 +- [ ] `tron/memory/` at `shared/memory/`; Spine role-memory still works side-by-side.                    
      830 +- [ ] `tron/parsers/` at `build/kg/parsers/`; KG indexer consumes them.                                  
      831 +                                                                                                         
      832 +**Canonical phase:**                                                                                     
      833 +- [ ] `sdlc-pipeline.yaml` default includes `verify` phase between `build` and `acceptance`.             
      834 +- [ ] Org bundle can override ISO agent selection (verified via `regulated-enterprise` test bundle).     
      835 +                                                                                                         
      836 +**ISO early-detect (SHOULD):**                                                                           
      837 +- [ ] Engineer daemon can call `iso_invoke('SecurityISO', ...)` mid-Build; results surface in Build repor
          +t.                                                                                                       
      838 +                                                                                                         
      839 +### 8.10. Related artifacts                                                                              
      840 +                                                                                                         
      841 +- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §5 (code mapping), §6 (migration phases), §8 (sprint sequencing)                
      842 +- `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-8 (6 EPICs, ~22 stories)                                                        
      843 +- `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-2 EPIC-2.4, INIT-3 EPIC-3.5/3.6/3.7 (overlapping lifts)                         
      844 +- `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-9 (caller), INIT-7 EPIC-7.4 (`BuildArtifact` input)                             
      845 +- `docs/PRD.md#req-init-7` (early-detect consumer), `#req-init-9` (caller)                               
      846 +- `verify/README.md` (subsystem boundary, standalone requirement)                                        
      847 +- TRON source: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Utilities/tron`                                             
      848 +- `docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md` — sixth corner of the moat                                    
      849 +                                                                                                         
      850  ---
      851  
      852  ## REQ-INIT-9

⏺ Update(docs/PRD.md)
  ⎿  Added 185 lines, removed 3 lines
       851  
       852  ## REQ-INIT-9
       853  
       854 -**Central Orchestrator**                                                                                
       854 +**Central Orchestrator — lifecycle, gates, routing, portfolio, unified cost+audit**                     
       855  
       856  | | |
       857  |---|---|
       858 -| **Status** | To write — see `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-9 epics for scope outline |                        
       858 +| **Status** | **Draft v1** (awaiting sign-off) |                                                       
       859 +| **Owner** | Khash Sarrafi |                                                                           
       860  | **Initiative** | `INIT-9` in `docs/BACKLOG.md` |
       861 +| **Last updated** | 2026-05-16 |                                                                       
       862 +| **Architecture** | `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §2 (orchestrator at top of diagram), §9 (locked tech decisio
           +ns) |                                                                                                   
       863 +| **Boundary doc** | `orchestrator/README.md` |                                                         
       864 +| **Tech decisions** | `memory/spine_tech_stack_decisions.md` (bash + Postgres core; no Redis; Temporal 
           +stays in verify/) |                                                                                     
       865  
       861 -*Placeholder.* Full PRD to be authored after Sprint 1 lifecycle schema lands so we have concrete state m
           -achine semantics to specify against. See `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-9 epics (`EPIC-9.1` through `EPIC-9.9`) 
           -for working scope: state machine, transition engine, gate enforcement, routing, portfolio, cost+audit ag
           -gregation, failure handling, API surface.                                                               
       866 +### 9.1. Summary                                                                                        
       867 +                                                                                                        
       868 +Build the **central coordinator** that turns Plan, Build, and Verify into one product. Orchestrator owns
           + the project lifecycle state machine, enforces phase gates with cryptographic approval tokens, dispatche
           +s directives to subsystems exclusively via MCP, aggregates cost and audit data into unified ledgers, run
           +s portfolio management for multiple in-flight projects, and exposes the single user-facing surface (MCP 
           ++ REST + CLI).                                                                                          
       869 +                                                                                                        
       870 +Implemented as **bash + Postgres** (preserves debuggability moat) with minimal Python helpers. State liv
           +es in `spine_lifecycle` Postgres schema. Pipeline-as-data driven — canonical phase set editable via `sdl
           +c-pipeline.yaml` (per `EPIC-1.7`), not hardcoded.                                                       
       871 +                                                                                                        
       872 +### 9.2. Problem                                                                                        
       873 +                                                                                                        
       874 +Without a central orchestrator, Spine v2 is **three disconnected things**: Plan produces a Roadmap into 
           +the void, Build executes against whatever directive lands, Verify runs whenever someone remembers to inv
           +oke it. Seven gaps:                                                                                     
       875 +                                                                                                        
       876 +- **No lifecycle state** — nobody knows which projects are in flight, in what phase, blocked on what.   
       877 +- **No enforced gates** — Plan can hand off to Build without sign-off; Verify findings can be silently i
           +gnored.                                                                                                 
       878 +- **No routing contract** — subsystems invoked ad hoc, no record of who asked for what.                 
       879 +- **No unified cost view** — Plan/Build/Verify tokens in three different ledgers.                       
       880 +- **No unified audit** — each subsystem writes its own format; reconstructing a decision crosses three l
           +ogs.                                                                                                    
       881 +- **No portfolio surface** — multi-project user has no place to see "what's where", no resource limits. 
       882 +- **No single user surface** — user learns three CLIs (or UIs) to drive one product.                    
       883 +                                                                                                        
       884 +Orchestrator closes all seven. It is the *spine* in Spine.                                              
       885 +                                                                                                        
       886 +### 9.3. Users & stakeholders                                                                           
       887 +                                                                                                        
       888 +| Stakeholder | What they need |                                                                        
       889 +|---|---|                                                                                               
       890 +| **Vibecoder** | Single front door: create project, see status, approve gates, watch cost — without kno
           +wing Plan/Build/Verify exist |                                                                          
       891 +| **Solo dev / power user** | CLI driving full lifecycle from terminal |                                
       892 +| **Plan / Build / Verify** | Clear dispatch contract over MCP; well-defined place to report results; fr
           +eedom from cross-subsystem coupling |                                                                   
       893 +| **Auditor / Compliance** | Single append-only audit log; cryptographically verifiable approval tokens 
           +|                                                                                                       
       894 +| **Finance / Procurement** | Unified cost ledger with per-phase/project/user/org rollups; budget enforc
           +ement reads aggregated spend |                                                                          
       895 +| **CTO / Eng manager** | Portfolio view: how many projects in each phase, which are blocked, which exce
           +ed retry budgets |                                                                                      
       896 +| **Org admins** | Confidence org-bundle-required gates cannot be bypassed |                            
       897 +                                                                                                        
       898 +### 9.4. Goals                                                                                          
       899 +                                                                                                        
       900 +#### MUST (P0)                                                                                          
       901 +                                                                                                        
       902 +- **G-1** `spine_lifecycle` Postgres schema with `project`, `phase`, `transition`, `approval`, `route_hi
           +story` tables.                                                                                          
       903 +- **G-2** Canonical phase set in `sdlc-pipeline.yaml` loaded at startup (default 11 phases: `intake → ..
           +. → retro`).                                                                                            
       904 +- **G-3** Bash-based state transition engine validating every transition against manifest, rejecting ski
           +ps, writing atomically.                                                                                 
       905 +- **G-4** Phase-gate enforcement with **HMAC-signed approval tokens** stored in `approval` table.       
       906 +- **G-5** Routing layer dispatching via MCP only (`plan_dispatch`, `build_dispatch`, `verify_audit`) — n
           +o direct imports.                                                                                       
       907 +- **G-6** Every dispatched directive carries **locked pipeline version**.                               
       908 +- **G-7** Unified cost ledger — Plan/Build/Verify rows in `spine_recording.costs` with `subsystem` colum
           +n.                                                                                                      
       909 +- **G-8** Unified audit log — append-only `spine_audit` table; every subsystem writes here.             
       910 +- **G-9** Verify-failure auto-remediation — failing result generates remediation directive routed back t
           +o Build.                                                                                                
       911 +- **G-10** User-facing API: MCP primitives + CLI (`spine project new|status|approve`).                  
       912 +                                                                                                        
       913 +#### SHOULD (P1)                                                                                        
       914 +                                                                                                        
       915 +- **G-11** Rollback support — transition can revert with recorded rationale.                            
       916 +- **G-12** Multi-approver gates (TRD requires CTO + Compliance both sign).                              
       917 +- **G-13** Portfolio mode — multiple projects with per-project context routing + resource limits.       
       918 +- **G-14** Max-retry policy per phase (default 3 build/verify loops before surfacing).                  
       919 +- **G-15** Build-failure → Plan re-route with "scope unclear" feedback.                                 
       920 +- **G-16** REST API (`/api/v2/projects`, `/api/v2/approvals`, `/api/v2/audit`).                         
       921 +- **G-17** Per-phase/project/user/org cost rollup SQL views.                                            
       922 +                                                                                                        
       923 +#### COULD (P2)                                                                                         
       924 +                                                                                                        
       925 +- **G-18** Cross-project rollups in dashboard.                                                          
       926 +- **G-19** Real-time dashboard tile.                                                                    
       927 +- **G-20** Audit export API for compliance.                                                             
       928 +                                                                                                        
       929 +#### WON'T                                                                                              
       930 +                                                                                                        
       931 +- **Subsystem internals** — those live in their own REQs.                                               
       932 +- **Temporal at orchestrator layer** — Temporal stays in `verify/` only.                                
       933 +- **Redis or any second state store** — Postgres is THE state store.                                    
       934 +- **Multi-tenant SaaS** — local-deploy only in v1.                                                      
       935 +- **Knowledge graph, standards/policy enforcement** — those live in `build/kg/` and `shared/standards/`.
       936 +                                                                                                        
       937 +### 9.5. Functional requirements                                                                        
       938 +                                                                                                        
       939 +#### FR-1 — Lifecycle state machine (Postgres schema)                                                   
       940 +                                                                                                        
       941 +Dedicated `spine_lifecycle` schema with five tables: `project` (id, name, project_type, pipeline_version
           + locked, current_phase, status, created_ts/by), `phase` (catalog loaded from manifest), `transition` (ap
           +pend-only history), `approval` (HMAC-signed tokens), `route_history` (every dispatch + reply). Migrated 
           +via existing `db/flyway/` tooling.                                                                      
       942 +                                                                                                        
       943 +#### FR-2 — Canonical phase set (pipeline-as-data)                                                      
       944 +                                                                                                        
       945 +Default 11 phases declared in `sdlc-pipeline.yaml`: `intake (user_approval) → plan_in_progress → plan_ap
           +proved (user_approval) → build_in_progress → build_complete (system) → verify_in_progress → verify_appro
           +ved (user_approval) → acceptance (user_approval) → released (system) → operate → retro (user_approval)`.
           + Editable per `EPIC-1.7` — authorized roles add/remove/reorder phases via manifest without touching sour
           +ce. Orchestrator loads manifest at startup and writes phase catalog into `spine_lifecycle.phase`.       
       946 +                                                                                                        
       947 +#### FR-3 — State transition engine                                                                     
       948 +                                                                                                        
       949 +`orchestrator/lib/transition.sh` (bash) + `orchestrator/lib/approval.py` (Python helper for HMAC verify)
           +. For each transition: (1) Read current phase from `project`. (2) Validate target is next ordinal phase 
           +or permitted rollback. (3) Verify gate conditions (required approvals + valid HMAC). (4) Begin Postgres 
           +transaction — update `current_phase`, insert into `transition`, insert into `route_history` (if dispatch
           +ing), insert into `spine_audit`. (5) Commit atomically.                                                 
       950 +                                                                                                        
       951 +Invalid transitions rejected with structured error referencing manifest phase ordering. No implicit phas
           +e skipping.                                                                                             
       952 +                                                                                                        
       953 +#### FR-4 — Phase gate enforcement (HMAC approval tokens)                                               
       954 +                                                                                                        
       955 +HMAC key in `~/.spine/secrets/hmac.key` (per-install, 256-bit). Token payload: `(project_id, phase, appr
           +over, ts)`; signature: `HMAC-SHA256(payload, key)`. Stored in `approval` table; presented on every `phas
           +e_advance`. Multi-approver gates (P1) require N valid tokens with distinct approvers per manifest declar
           +ation.                                                                                                  
       956 +                                                                                                        
       957 +#### FR-5 — Routing layer (MCP only)                                                                    
       958 +                                                                                                        
       959 +Subsystems addressed **exclusively via MCP** — no direct imports, no cross-subsystem shell calls. Three 
           +dispatch tools via `shared/mcp/`: `plan_dispatch` (`{project_id, phase, directive, pipeline_version}`), 
           +`build_dispatch` (`{project_id, directive, story_id?, pipeline_version, prior_findings?}`), `verify_audi
           +t` (`{project_id, artifact_ref, scope, pipeline_version}`). Every directive carries `pipeline_version` (
           +per `EPIC-1.7.5`) so mid-flight edits don't affect in-flight projects. Subsystems reply via MCP; orchest
           +rator records reply in `route_history` + audit row.                                                     
       960 +                                                                                                        
       961 +#### FR-6 — Portfolio management                                                                        
       962 +                                                                                                        
       963 +Orchestrator runs N projects concurrently. Per-project context keyed by `project_id`. Per-project resour
           +ce limits (`max_parallel_directives`, `max_workers`) declared in org bundle or project metadata; exceede
           +d requests queue. Cross-project rollups via SQL views: `v_projects_by_phase`, `v_blocked_projects`, `v_a
           +ctive_directives`. Per-project resource accounting rolls into unified cost ledger.                      
       964 +                                                                                                        
       965 +#### FR-7 — Unified cost ledger                                                                         
       966 +                                                                                                        
       967 +All cost rows from all subsystems land in `spine_recording.costs` with added `subsystem` column (`plan`/
           +`build`/`verify`/`orchestrator`). Orchestrator doesn't own the cost recorder (that's `shared/cost/`) but
           + mandates the contract. Rollup views (P1): `v_cost_per_project`, `v_cost_per_user`, `v_cost_per_org`, `v
           +_cost_per_pipeline_version`. Budget enforcement (per `EPIC-2.3`) reads these views, blocks dispatch when
           + caps exhausted.                                                                                        
       968 +                                                                                                        
       969 +#### FR-8 — Unified audit log                                                                           
       970 +                                                                                                        
       971 +Append-only `spine_audit` table: `(ts, project_id, phase, role, subsystem, action, subject_id, rationale
           +, prompt_hash?, output_hash?, cost?, pipeline_version, audit_id)`. Append-only enforced by Postgres role
           + (no UPDATE/DELETE grants). Survives uninstall — table is in durable `db/` instance. Queryable via compl
           +iance export API (P1): given `project_id`, return full chronological audit trail.                       
       972 +                                                                                                        
       973 +#### FR-9 — Failure handling & re-routing                                                               
       974 +                                                                                                        
       975 +Verify failure → Build re-route. When `verify_audit` returns failing findings, orchestrator auto-generat
           +es remediation directive (`{prior_findings, scope, fix_target}`), dispatches back to Build, records loop
           + in `route_history`. Build failure → Plan re-route ("scope unclear" feedback). Max-retry policy per phas
           +e (default 3); exceeded → project transitions to `blocked` status. All loops are first-class state — re-
           +routes write `transition` rows (annotated `rollback=true` where applicable).                            
       976 +                                                                                                        
       977 +#### FR-10 — API surface (MCP + REST + CLI)                                                             
       978 +                                                                                                        
       979 +| Surface | Tools / endpoints / commands |                                                              
       980 +|---|---|                                                                                               
       981 +| **MCP (P0)** | `project_create`, `project_status`, `phase_advance`, `approval_grant`, `plan_dispatch`,
           + `build_dispatch`, `verify_audit`, `audit_query` |                                                      
       982 +| **REST (P1)** | `POST /api/v2/projects`, `GET /api/v2/projects/{id}`, `POST /api/v2/approvals`, `GET /
           +api/v2/audit?project_id=...` |                                                                          
       983 +| **CLI (P1)** | `spine project new <name> [--type=...]`, `spine project status [<id>]`, `spine project 
           +approve <id> <phase>`, `spine project rollback <id>` |                                                  
       984 +                                                                                                        
       985 +MCP is canonical; REST and CLI are thin wrappers. Dashboard tile (P2) consumes REST.                    
       986 +                                                                                                        
       987 +### 9.6. Non-functional requirements                                                                    
       988 +                                                                                                        
       989 +- **NFR-1 Latency:** State transition (validate + write + audit) ≤ 100ms p95. MCP dispatch envelope (orc
           +hestrator overhead, not counting subsystem work) ≤ 50ms p95. `project_status` ≤ 200ms p95.              
       990 +- **NFR-2 Throughput:** Audit log write ≥ 500 rows/sec sustained. Concurrent in-flight projects ≥ 50 on 
           +single-instance install.                                                                                
       991 +- **NFR-3 Reliability:** Every transition atomic via Postgres ACID. Audit log append-only and survives u
           +ninstall. HMAC keys at `~/.spine/secrets/`, mode 0600, never logged. Mid-transition crash: Postgres roll
           +s back; `current_phase` reflects last committed transition on restart.                                  
       992 +- **NFR-4 Security:** Approval tokens HMAC-signed; tampering detected at gate-check. Postgres role for o
           +rchestrator has INSERT-only on `spine_audit` (no UPDATE/DELETE). No outbound calls from orchestrator lay
           +er itself.                                                                                              
       993 +- **NFR-5 Observability:** Every transition emits audit row with `actor`, `rationale`, `pipeline_version
           +`, correlation ID. `route_history` exposes mean dispatch-to-reply latency per subsystem.                
       994 +                                                                                                        
       995 +### 9.7. Dependencies                                                                                   
       996 +                                                                                                        
       997 +- **Existing Postgres** (`db/`, moves to `shared/db/` in Phase 2) — hosts new `spine_lifecycle` alongsid
           +e `spine_recording`, `spine_kg`, `spine_audit`.                                                         
       998 +- **`EPIC-2.2`** — Unified MCP server is the dispatch transport.                                        
       999 +- **`INIT-3`** — Audit log primitives.                                                                  
      1000 +- **`EPIC-1.7`** — Pipeline-as-data manifest supplies canonical phase set + override hierarchy.         
      1001 +- **`EPIC-1.4`** — Approval system.                                                                     
      1002 +- **`EPIC-2.3`** — Budget enforcement reads unified cost ledger.                                        
      1003 +- **`REQ-INIT-1`, `REQ-INIT-7`, `REQ-INIT-8`** — Dispatch targets; their MCP contracts must be implement
           +ed before end-to-end flow works.                                                                        
      1004 +                                                                                                        
      1005 +### 9.8. Open questions                                                                                 
      1006 +                                                                                                        
      1007 +- **OQ-1:** Bash/Python boundary inside orchestrator. Initial split: bash for transition engine, dispatc
           +h, CLI; Python for HMAC + structured payload validation. Revisit if Python creeps past ~500 LOC.        
      1008 +- **OQ-2:** HMAC key management. Single per-install key in v1; defer rotation + per-approver keypairs to
           + v1.1.                                                                                                  
      1009 +- **OQ-3:** Multi-tenant later. Add `tenant_id` columns with default `'local'` from day one (cheap insur
           +ance).                                                                                                  
      1010 +- **OQ-4:** Rollback semantics. Approvals remain valid after rollback (scoped to phase, not attempt); ma
           +nifest can override per gate.                                                                           
      1011 +- **OQ-5:** Phase set evolution. Locked projects stay on locked version; user-triggered re-lock with dif
           +f preview supported; never auto-migrate.                                                                
      1012 +                                                                                                        
      1013 +### 9.9. Acceptance criteria                                                                            
      1014 +                                                                                                        
      1015 +User (vibecoder):                                                                                       
      1016 +- [ ] `spine project new my-idea --type=web-app` → project appears in `spine_lifecycle.project` at `inta
           +ke`.                                                                                                    
      1017 +- [ ] `spine project status my-idea` shows current phase, last transition, pending approvals, total cost
           +.                                                                                                       
      1018 +- [ ] `spine project approve my-idea plan_approved` → project advances to `build_in_progress` with audit
           + row + HMAC token.                                                                                      
      1019 +- [ ] Verify failure auto-routes back to Build (`route_history` shows two `build_dispatch` rows for same
           + `project_id`, second carrying `prior_findings`) — no manual intervention.                              
      1020 +- [ ] `GET /api/v2/audit?project_id=my-idea` returns complete chronological reconstruction.             
      1021 +                                                                                                        
      1022 +Admin / operator:                                                                                       
      1023 +- [ ] Run two concurrent projects, isolated in `spine_lifecycle`, per-project resource limits queue disp
           +atches when exceeded.                                                                                   
      1024 +- [ ] Tamper with approval token → next `phase_advance` rejects with HMAC verification error.           
      1025 +- [ ] Edit `sdlc-pipeline.yaml` to insert new phase, restart orchestrator → in-flight projects continue 
           +against locked version; new projects pick up new phase.                                                 
      1026 +- [ ] Run `uninstall` → `spine_audit` rows survive in durable Postgres.                                 
      1027 +- [ ] `SELECT * FROM v_cost_per_project WHERE project_id=...` shows Plan + Build + Verify costs with `su
           +bsystem` column.                                                                                        
      1028 +                                                                                                        
      1029 +Subsystem:                                                                                              
      1030 +- [ ] Receives directive only via MCP tool — no direct imports from orchestrator.                       
      1031 +- [ ] Reports back via MCP; observe corresponding `route_history` row + audit row within latency NFR.   
      1032 +- [ ] Receives directive with `pipeline_version` and refuses if local manifest doesn't match.           
      1033 +                                                                                                        
      1034 +### 9.10. Related artifacts                                                                             
      1035 +                                                                                                        
      1036 +- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §2, §9                                                                         
      1037 +- `docs/BACKLOG.md` INIT-9 (`EPIC-9.1` through `EPIC-9.9`)                                              
      1038 +- `orchestrator/README.md`                                                                              
      1039 +- `docs/PRD.md#req-init-1` / `#req-init-7` / `#req-init-8` (dispatch targets)                           
      1040 +- `memory/spine_tech_stack_decisions.md` (bash + Postgres; no Redis; Temporal only in verify/)          
      1041 +- `memory/spine_flexibility_principle.md` (pipeline-as-data)                                            
      1042 +- `EPIC-1.7` (pipeline customization), `EPIC-2.2` (MCP server), `EPIC-2.3` (budget enforcement)         
      1043 +- `INIT-3` (audit log primitives)

   foundation. Eight parallel agents implemented orchestrator extensions,
   shared infrastructure, KG extractors, eval harness, and TRON integration
   wrappers.

     reject/request-changes/list-pending; wraps approval.py + transition.sh
     + router.sh). STORY-1.4.1, 1.4.4, 1.4.5.

     (compose/check-retry/dispatch/surface; reads phases.yaml retry policy).
     STORY-9.8.1, 9.8.2.

  Plan/Build/Verify-wiring (the actual happy-path runtime) is the next gap.

  Two paths forward

  Option A — Wave 4 (continue parallel design): 8 more agents on:
  - STORY-1.1.1 full 5-move protocol in product.md (the intake feature itself)
  - STORY-1.2.1 swarm orchestration LangGraph subgraph

  - STORY-1.5.2 per-turn escalation classifier
  - STORY-1.4.2 approval queue UI (frontend)
  - STORY-7.1.2 build subsystem boundary check CI script
  
  Option B — Pause for review: stop here, you review what's been built, then decide what to prioritize. Three commits to
  read; can git log -p any of them.

  Per your "continue orchestrating" directive I'll default to Option A unless you push back. Spawning wave 4 in next

❯ Implement Spine's REST API for the orchestrator. Implements `STORY-9.9.2` (REST API `/api/v2/projects`,                  
  `/api/v2/approvals`, `/api/v2/audit`) in `docs/BACKLOG.md`. Replaces the dev-only `shared/ui/approvals/proxy.py` with a  
  proper FastAPI service.                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  The REST API is a thin wrapper over the unified MCP server + direct Postgres reads. UI (current dev:                     
  `shared/ui/approvals/`; future: `shared/ui/dashboard/`) consumes it. Stays in `shared/api/`. FastAPI (TRON's stack —     
  already in `verify/`). Python 3.11+.                                                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-9 §9.5 FR-10 (API surface table — REST routes listed)                                          
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — STORY-9.9.2                                                                                       
  3. `shared/mcp/server.py` + `shared/mcp/tools/orchestrator.py` — the MCP tools this wraps                                
  4. `orchestrator/lib/gate.sh` — the gate API the `/approvals` endpoints proxy to                                         
  5. `shared/ui/approvals/proxy.py` — existing dev proxy; the FastAPI replaces it                                          
  6. `db/flyway/sql/V14__spine_lifecycle_schema.sql` — schema for direct reads                                             
  7. `db/flyway/sql/V15__spine_audit_schema.sql` — audit query schema                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — write FILES in `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/shared/api/`                              
                                                                                                                           
  Create directory.                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  1. `__init__.py` (~5 lines)                                                                                              
  2. `app.py` (~200 lines) — FastAPI app + route registration                                                              
  3. `routes/__init__.py` (~10 lines)                                                                                      
  4. `routes/projects.py` (~180 lines) — `/api/v2/projects`                                                                
  5. `routes/approvals.py` (~150 lines) — `/api/v2/approvals`                                                              
  6. `routes/audit.py` (~120 lines) — `/api/v2/audit`                                                                      
  7. `dependencies.py` (~80 lines) — shared FastAPI dependencies (DB, MCP, auth stub)                                      
  8. `README.md` (~100 lines) — routes table, run instructions, auth model                                                 
                                                                                                                           
  ## REST routes (per FR-10 + UI needs)                                                                                    
                                                                                                                           
  ### Projects                                                                                                             
  - `POST /api/v2/projects` — create (delegates to MCP `project_create`)                                                   
  - `GET /api/v2/projects` — list (query params: `phase`, `status`, `owner`, `limit`, `offset`)                            
  - `GET /api/v2/projects/{project_id}` — detail (phase history, transitions, pending approvals, cost)                     
  - `PATCH /api/v2/projects/{project_id}` — update metadata (status: paused, terminated)                                   
  - `POST /api/v2/projects/{project_id}/phase-advance` — delegates to MCP `phase_advance`                                  
  - `POST /api/v2/projects/{project_id}/rollback` — rollback transition                                                    
                                                                                                                           
  ### Approvals                                                                                                            
  - `GET /api/v2/approvals?status=pending|approved|rejected&since=24h` — list                                              
  - `POST /api/v2/approvals` — body `{project_id, phase, action: approve|reject|request_changes, approver, notes?}`        
  (delegates to `gate.sh`)                                                                                                 
  - `GET /api/v2/approvals/{approval_id}` — detail                                                                         
                                                                                                                           
  ### Audit                                                                                                                
  - `GET /api/v2/audit?project_id=X` — chronological trail                                                                 
  - `GET /api/v2/audit?correlation_id=X` — reconstruct one flow                                                            
  - `GET /api/v2/audit/export?project_id=X&format=csv|json` — export                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  ### Health + misc                                                                                                        
  - `GET /healthz` — DB + MCP reachability                                                                                 
  - `GET /readyz` — additional checks (schema migrations applied)                                                          
  - `GET /api/v2/spec` — OpenAPI JSON                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## app.py requirements                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                           
  - FastAPI app with CORS middleware (configurable origins via env `SPINE_API_CORS_ORIGINS`, default                       
  `http://localhost:8080`)                                                                                                 
  - Mount route modules                                                                                                    
  - Lifespan handler: open Postgres conn pool on startup, close on shutdown                                                
  - Configure JSON logging (no secrets/tokens)                                                                             
  - Add request-id middleware (X-Request-ID header)                                                                        
  - Health endpoints + OpenAPI spec available                                                                              
  - Pydantic v2 response models (re-use shared/audit + lifecycle schemas where possible)                                   
                                                                                                                           
  ## Route module pattern                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  Each route module:                                                                                                       
  - Defines `router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v2/...", tags=[...])`                                                         
  - Pydantic input/output models                                                                                           
  - Dependency injection via `Depends(get_db_pool)` etc.                                                                   
  - Routes call into MCP tools for mutations + direct psql for reads                                                       
  - Standardized error responses: `{"error_code": str, "message": str, "details": dict?}`                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## dependencies.py                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  - `get_db_pool()` — singleton Postgres pool (use asyncpg or psycopg pool; default to subprocess psql wrapper if neither  
  installed)                                                                                                               
  - `get_mcp_client()` — wrapper that POSTs to local MCP server                                                            
  - `current_user(request)` — stub returning a default identity (auth is a v2 story)                                       
                                                                                                                           
  ## Auth notes for v1                                                                                                     
  - No auth required for v1 (single-user local-deploy)                                                                     
  - Auth hooks live in `current_user()` — easy to swap in JWT/OAuth later                                                  
  - All requests log the identity (default "local-user") + request_id                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## Style                                                                                                                 
  - Python 3.11+, FastAPI ≥ 0.110, Pydantic v2                                                                             
  - `from __future__ import annotations`                                                                                   
  - Type-hint everything                                                                                                   
  - ≤200 app.py, ≤180 routes/projects.py, ≤150 routes/approvals.py, ≤120 routes/audit.py, ≤80 dependencies.py, ≤100 README 
  - Reuse Pydantic models from `shared/audit/audit_record.py` and `shared/schemas/build/build_artifact.py` where           
  applicable                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Edit files outside `shared/api/`                                                                                       
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Implement actual auth                                                                                                  
  - Try to run the app                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  Report back with all 8 file paths.
