❯ Implement Spine's Technical Review Swarm primitive — the orchestration pattern where the architect dispatches scoped     
  sub-directives to swarm members (researcher / engineer / datawright / operator / qa), collects their per-lens            
  contributions, synthesizes them into a single TRD. Implements `STORY-1.2.1` (swarm orchestration primitive) and          
  `STORY-1.2.4` (per-scout contribution format + synthesis pattern) in `docs/BACKLOG.md`. This is the **biggest            
  architectural feature still missing** — uses LangGraph per `EPIC-1.2` tech note.                                         
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  Today the architect role works solo on TRD synthesis. The swarm pattern fans out to specialist scouts who each           
  contribute their lens (researcher: current system shape; engineer: feasibility; datawright: data; operator: infra; qa:   
  quality concerns). Architect synthesizes. LangGraph subgraph inside the architect daemon manages the                     
  fan-out/collect/synthesize lifecycle with checkpointing.                                                                 
                                                                                                                           
  LangGraph is optional Python dep used inside specific roles (per `memory/spine_tech_stack_decisions.md`). This is one of 
   the planned use sites.                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-1 §1.5 FR-3 (Technical Review swarm — full)                                                    
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — INIT-1 EPIC-1.2 (all 5 stories)                                                                   
  3. `plan/artifacts/trd_v1.py` — TRD schema the swarm produces                                                            
  4. `plan/artifacts/sdlc-pipeline-default.yaml` — per-project-type swarm composition rules                                
  5. `lib/role-prompts/architect.md` — current architect role; you'll add a "swarm mode" section                           
  6. `memory/spine_tech_stack_decisions.md` — LangGraph usage notes                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — write FILES in `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/plan/swarm/`                              
                                                                                                                           
  Create directory.                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  1. `__init__.py` (~10 lines)                                                                                             
  2. `swarm_engine.py` (~280 lines) — LangGraph-based swarm orchestration                                                  
  3. `scout_contribution.py` (~150 lines) — Pydantic schema for per-scout output                                           
  4. `synthesis.py` (~180 lines) — architect synthesis from scout contributions → TRD                                      
  5. `composition_rules.py` (~100 lines) — per-project-type swarm membership resolver                                      
  6. `swarm_README.md` (~120 lines) — architecture + flow diagram + how to add a scout                                     
                                                                                                                           
  ## swarm_engine.py requirements                                                                                          
                                                                                                                           
  LangGraph-based subgraph (assume `langgraph` installed; lazy import to allow modules to load without it).                
                                                                                                                           
  **Graph nodes:**                                                                                                         
  1. `start` — receive `(prd, project_type, locked_pipeline_version)`                                                      
  2. `compose_swarm` — read `composition_rules.py` to determine which scouts to dispatch                                   
  3. `dispatch_scouts` — fan out: for each scout role, write a directive to `teams/<role>/directive.md` (existing Spine    
  pattern) with PRD + scope; OR call MCP tool `plan_dispatch` per scout                                                    
  4. `wait_for_scouts` — poll for scout completion; collect `ScoutContribution` from each                                  
  5. `synthesize` — invoke `synthesis.py` to merge contributions into a TRD                                                
  6. `validate_trd` — Pydantic validation against `trd-v1` schema                                                          
  7. `end` — return TRD + provenance (which scouts ran, their contributions)                                               
                                                                                                                           
  **Checkpointing:**                                                                                                       
  - Use LangGraph's `MemorySaver` or `SqliteSaver` checkpointer (SqliteSaver if available; falls back to in-memory)        
  - Persist state at every node boundary                                                                                   
  - Resume from checkpoint: if scout fan-out completed but synthesis didn't, can pick up at synthesis                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Failure handling:**                                                                                                    
  - Scout failure: log + mark `unrun`; continue with remaining scouts (degraded TRD with `open_questions` flagging the     
  missing lens)                                                                                                            
  - Synthesis failure: retry once with a simpler prompt; if still fails, escalate to user                                  
  - Validation failure: send back to synthesis with the validation errors                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  **CLI:** `python -m plan.swarm.swarm_engine --prd <path> --project-type <type> [--resume <checkpoint_id>]` — for         
  standalone testing                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  ## scout_contribution.py                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                           
  Pydantic v2 model `ScoutContribution`:                                                                                   
  - `scout_role` (researcher/engineer/datawright/operator/qa)                                                              
  - `scope_received` (PRD section the scout was asked to assess)                                                           
  - `lens` (their per-role concern: feasibility / data / infra / quality / current-state)                                  
  - `findings` — list of structured items: `(severity, kind, file_or_section, description, recommendation)`                
  - `risks` — list with likelihood/impact                                                                                  
  - `open_questions` — questions the architect should resolve before TRD synthesis                                         
  - `confidence` (0-1; how confident the scout is in their analysis)                                                       
  - `cost_usd` + `duration_ms` + `model_used`                                                                              
                                                                                                                           
  ## synthesis.py                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                           
  `synthesize_trd(prd: PRDv1, contributions: list[ScoutContribution]) -> TRDv1`:                                           
  1. Group contributions by lens                                                                                           
  2. Build TRD sections from contribution maps:                                                                            
     - architecture ← engineer + researcher merged                                                                         
     - data_model ← datawright (if present)                                                                                
     - integrations ← engineer + operator                                                                                  
     - nfrs ← qa + operator + engineer                                                                                     
     - tech_choices ← engineer                                                                                             
     - risks ← all scouts unioned + deduped                                                                                
     - open_questions ← all scouts unioned                                                                                 
     - scope_estimate ← engineer + planner heuristic                                                                       
     - cost_projection ← engineer + datawright                                                                             
  3. LLM call to synthesize prose for each section (uses cost router's `medium` tier; escalate via classifier if needed)   
  4. Validate TRD via Pydantic; if validation fails return errors for swarm_engine retry                                   
  5. Return TRDv1 instance                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                           
  ## composition_rules.py                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  Reads `plan/artifacts/sdlc-pipeline-default.yaml` for per-project-type swarm composition:                                
  - `get_swarm_for(project_type) -> list[str]` returns scout roles                                                         
  - Defaults from FR-3: web_app → [researcher, engineer, operator, qa]; data_pipeline → adds datawright; mobile → adds     
  operator (distribution); etc.                                                                                            
  - Override-able via org bundle (read `~/.spine/active/team` or `org` bundle's `pipeline_overrides`)                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## swarm_README.md                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  - Why swarm pattern (architect can't be expert in all lenses)                                                            
  - ASCII diagram of the LangGraph state flow                                                                              
  - The 5 scout roles + their lenses                                                                                       
  - How synthesis works (per-lens grouping + LLM synthesis per TRD section)                                                
  - Failure modes + resumption                                                                                             
  - How to add a new scout (e.g., security-scout, ux-scout)                                                                
  - Cross-refs: REQ-INIT-1 FR-3, EPIC-1.2, trd_v1.py, architect.md                                                         
                                                                                                                           
  ## Constraints                                                                                                           
  - Python 3.11+, Pydantic v2, LangGraph optional (lazy import)                                                            
  - ≤280 swarm_engine, ≤150 scout_contribution, ≤180 synthesis, ≤100 composition_rules, ≤120 README                        
  - Standalone testable (no Spine runtime required)                                                                        
  - Don't modify architect.md in this story — that's a follow-up integration story                                         
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Edit files outside `plan/swarm/`                                                                                       
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Modify role prompts                                                                                                    
  - Try to run with a real PRD                                                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  Report back with all 6 file paths.

❯ Implement Spine's Roadmap Decomposer — the planner-led pipeline that reads PRD + TRD and emits a Roadmap                 
  (INIT/EPIC/STORY hierarchy). Implements `STORY-1.3.1` (decomposer playbook), `STORY-1.3.2` (story sizing heuristic), and 
   `STORY-1.3.3` (inter-story dependency detection via KG) in `docs/BACKLOG.md`.                                           
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  After PRD + TRD are signed, the planner role reads them and produces a Roadmap (INIT → EPIC → STORY with stable IDs).    
  Decomposer uses KG `impact_radius` + `code_neighborhood` for deterministic story-dependency detection (replaces          
  heuristic placeholder).                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-1 §1.5 FR-4 (decomposer — full)                                                                
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — INIT-1 EPIC-1.3 stories                                                                           
  3. `plan/artifacts/prd_v1.py`, `trd_v1.py`, `roadmap_v1.py` — schemas                                                    
  4. `shared/mcp/tools/kg.py` — `impact_radius`, `code_neighborhood` (use these for dependency detection)                  
  5. `lib/role-prompts/planner.md` — current planner role                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — write FILES in `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/plan/decomposer/`                         
                                                                                                                           
  Create directory.                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  1. `__init__.py` (~10 lines)                                                                                             
  2. `decomposer.py` (~280 lines) — main playbook                                                                          
  3. `sizing.py` (~150 lines) — STORY-1.3.2 story sizing heuristic                                                         
  4. `dependency_detection.py` (~180 lines) — STORY-1.3.3 inter-story dependency via KG                                    
  5. `id_allocator.py` (~80 lines) — stable INIT/EPIC/STORY ID allocation                                                  
  6. `decomposer_README.md` (~120 lines) — algorithm + outputs                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## decomposer.py requirements                                                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  `decompose(prd: PRDv1, trd: TRDv1, existing_roadmap: RoadmapV1 | None = None) -> RoadmapV1`:                             
                                                                                                                           
  1. **Build initiative shape** from PRD goals (MUST → P0, SHOULD → P1, COULD → P2). One initiative per major problem      
  cluster.                                                                                                                 
  2. **Build epics from TRD** — each TRD architecture component / integration / NFR becomes an epic candidate. Cluster     
  related items.                                                                                                           
  3. **Build stories** — break epics into concrete deliverables (single PR scale, ≤2 weeks). Use TRD `scope_estimate` as a 
   guide.                                                                                                                  
  4. **Allocate IDs** via `id_allocator.py` — must be stable across decompose() invocations (idempotent on                 
  re-decomposition with same inputs).                                                                                      
  5. **Size each story** via `sizing.py`.                                                                                  
  6. **Detect dependencies** via `dependency_detection.py`.                                                                
  7. **Sequence into sprints** — topological sort by deps; aim for ≤8 stories per sprint, ≤3 sprints by default.           
  8. **Validate** via Pydantic `RoadmapV1`.                                                                                
  9. **Return** + emit markdown via `roadmap_v1.to_markdown()` for direct BACKLOG-style export.                            
                                                                                                                           
  If `existing_roadmap` provided: incremental decomposition — preserve existing story IDs, mark new stories, retire        
  orphaned stories.                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  ## sizing.py                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  `estimate_size(story_text: str, trd_section: dict | None, kg_impact: list[str] | None) -> Size`:                         
  - Heuristics: lines of expected code change, file count touched, presence of new external integration, novel data model, 
   security implications                                                                                                   
  - Returns `XS` (<1 day) / `S` (1-3 days) / `M` (1-2 weeks) / `L` (3-6 weeks) / `XL` (release-scale)                      
  - Plus `estimated_cost_usd` rough projection: XS=$1, S=$5, M=$25, L=$100, XL=$500 (placeholder rates)                    
  - Plus `estimated_duration_hours` matching the size bucket                                                               
                                                                                                                           
  ## dependency_detection.py                                                                                               
                                                                                                                           
  `detect_dependencies(stories: list[Story], kg_available: bool = True) -> list[StoryDependency]`:                         
                                                                                                                           
  1. For each pair (A, B) of stories:                                                                                      
     - If both reference same code regions (use `find_by_satisfies` MCP tool or text overlap fallback): possible           
  dependency                                                                                                               
     - Call `impact_radius` on story A's target code: if story B's target is in A's impact set: B depends on A             
     - If story A modifies a function and story B tests it: B depends on A                                                 
     - If story A modifies a schema and story B uses it: B depends on A                                                    
  2. Cycle detection — flag circular deps                                                                                  
  3. Return `list[StoryDependency(from_story_id, to_story_id, reason, confidence)]`                                        
  4. If `kg_available=False`: fall back to text-overlap heuristic (warn that dependencies are best-effort)                 
                                                                                                                           
  ## id_allocator.py                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  `allocate_ids(initiatives, existing_roadmap=None) -> dict[str, str]`:                                                    
  - Stable: same input always produces same IDs                                                                            
  - Hash-based (sha256 of canonical content) → integer suffix                                                              
  - Format: `INIT-{N}`, `EPIC-{N}.{M}`, `STORY-{N}.{M}.{K}`                                                                
  - If existing_roadmap provided: preserve IDs of unchanged content; only allocate new IDs for new content                 
  - Return: `dict[canonical_content_hash -> id]`                                                                           
                                                                                                                           
  ## decomposer_README.md                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  - Algorithm (high level)                                                                                                 
  - Inputs (PRD + TRD + optional existing roadmap)                                                                         
  - Outputs (Roadmap with stable IDs + sprint plan)                                                                        
  - How sizing works                                                                                                       
  - How dependency detection works (KG path + fallback)                                                                    
  - How to use incremental mode (pass existing_roadmap to preserve IDs)                                                    
  - Integration with `planner` role daemon (planner invokes `decompose()` after receiving signed PRD+TRD)                  
  - Cross-refs: STORY-1.3.1/2/3, EPIC-1.3, roadmap_v1.py, kg.py                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  ## Constraints                                                                                                           
  - Python 3.11+, Pydantic v2, stdlib + pydantic + (lazy KG MCP subprocess calls if available)                             
  - ≤280 decomposer, ≤150 sizing, ≤180 dependency_detection, ≤80 id_allocator, ≤120 README                                 
  - Idempotent on stable inputs                                                                                            
  - Don't modify roadmap_v1.py                                                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Edit files outside `plan/decomposer/`                                                                                  
  - Modify planner.md                                                                                                      
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Try to actually decompose a real PRD                                                                                   
                                                                                                                           
  Report back with all 6 file paths.

❯ Implement Spine's Knowledge Graph incremental indexer — the engine that fills `spine_kg.kg_node` and `kg_edge` tables    
  from the codebase. Implements `STORY-6.4.1` (extend db/watcher/), `STORY-6.4.2` (diff-based update), and `STORY-6.4.3`   
  (cold-start full index) in `docs/BACKLOG.md`.                                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  The KG schema exists (V2 migration). Tree-sitter extractors are configured at `build/kg/extractors/*.yaml`. This story   
  builds the indexer that:                                                                                                 
  - Cold-starts: walks the entire repo, parses every supported file, inserts nodes + edges                                 
  - Incremental: on git commits, parses only changed files, computes node/edge diff, applies `INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE`        
  - Extends the existing `db/watcher/` directory (look at it for pattern reference)                                        
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-6 §6.5 FR-5 (incremental indexer spec)                                                         
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — EPIC-6.4 (3 stories)                                                                              
  3. `db/flyway/sql/V2__spine_kg_schema.sql` — node/edge tables                                                            
  4. `db/flyway/sql/V2__spine_kg_schema.README.md` — point-in-time semantics                                               
  5. `build/kg/extractors/_schema.yaml` — extractor config format                                                          
  6. `build/kg/extractors/python.yaml`, `bash.yaml`, `markdown.yaml` — examples                                            
  7. `db/watcher/` — list the directory; understand existing watcher pattern (you EXTEND this, don't replace)              
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — write FILES in `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/build/kg/indexer/`                        
                                                                                                                           
  Create directory.                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  1. `__init__.py` (~10 lines)                                                                                             
  2. `indexer.py` (~300 lines) — main indexer (cold-start + incremental)                                                   
  3. `parser_runtime.py` (~250 lines) — tree-sitter runtime that consumes extractor configs                                
  4. `diff_engine.py` (~180 lines) — diff old/new node/edge sets                                                           
  5. `watcher_extension.py` (~120 lines) — hooks into existing `db/watcher/`                                               
  6. `cli.py` (~120 lines) — CLI: `kg-index cold-start | incremental | status`                                             
  7. `indexer_README.md` (~120 lines) — algorithm + how to wire into git hooks                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## indexer.py requirements                                                                                               
                                                                                                                           
  **`cold_start_index(repo_root: Path, languages: list[str] | None = None) -> IndexResult`:**                              
  1. Walk repo (respect `file_filters` from each extractor config)                                                         
  2. For each file: select extractor by extension; parse via `parser_runtime.parse_file()`                                 
  3. Batch-INSERT nodes + edges into `spine_kg.kg_node` + `kg_edge` (batches of 1000)                                      
  4. Update `spine_kg.kg_index_state` with the commit SHA at start                                                         
  5. Target: ≤5min per 100k LOC (NFR-1 in REQ-INIT-6)                                                                      
  6. Return: `IndexResult(node_count, edge_count, files_indexed, duration_seconds, errors)`                                
                                                                                                                           
  **`incremental_index(commit_sha: str | None = None) -> IndexResult`:**                                                   
  1. Read `last_indexed_commit_sha` from `kg_index_state`                                                                  
  2. Run `git diff --name-status <last_indexed>..HEAD` to get changed files                                                
  3. For each changed file:                                                                                                
     - `A` (added) → parse, insert new nodes/edges                                                                         
     - `M` (modified) → parse new state, diff against existing (close old via `valid_to=NOW()`, insert new)                
     - `D` (deleted) → close all nodes/edges for that file with `valid_to=NOW()`                                           
  4. Update `kg_index_state.last_indexed_commit_sha` to HEAD                                                               
  5. Target: ≤5s for 10-file commit (NFR-1)                                                                                
  6. Return same `IndexResult` shape                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  **`reindex_file(file_path: Path) -> IndexResult`:** force re-parse single file (for testing / manual repair)             
                                                                                                                           
  ## parser_runtime.py                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  - Load extractor config (YAML) for a language                                                                            
  - Use `tree-sitter` Python bindings (lazy import; fall back to subprocess `tree-sitter` CLI if Python bindings           
  unavailable)                                                                                                             
  - Walk AST per extractor config `node_extractors` and `edge_extractors`                                                  
  - Resolve `from_resolver` / `to_resolver` per `_schema.yaml` patterns (enclosing_function, enclosing_module,             
  symbol_lookup, etc.)                                                                                                     
  - Emit `(nodes, edges)` lists where each is a dict matching `kg_node`/`kg_edge` column shape                             
  - Synthetic AST tokens (`__file_root__`, `__self__`, `__module_docstring__`, `__relpath_dotted__`, `__is_async__`,       
  `__heading_level__`, `__match__`, `__infer_from_path__`) handled at runtime                                              
  - Unresolved symbols emit edge to a synthetic `ExternalSymbol` node (per extractor README)                               
                                                                                                                           
  ## diff_engine.py                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  `diff_file_index(old_nodes, old_edges, new_nodes, new_edges) -> (insert_nodes, insert_edges, close_node_ids,             
  close_edge_ids)`:                                                                                                        
  - Match nodes by `node_id` (stable external ID); changes = close old + insert new (point-in-time supersede pattern)      
  - Match edges by `(from_node_id, to_node_id, type)`; same supersede pattern                                              
  - Return four lists for the indexer to apply transactionally                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## watcher_extension.py                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  Hook into existing `db/watcher/` daemon:                                                                                 
  - Subscribe to git commit events (via post-commit hook OR polling `git log` from a watcher loop)                         
  - On commit detected: call `incremental_index(commit_sha)`                                                               
  - Log results to `kg_index_state` + emit a structured log                                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## cli.py                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ```                                                                                                                      
  kg-index cold-start [--repo <path>] [--languages py,ts,bash,md]                                                          
  kg-index incremental [--repo <path>]                                                                                     
  kg-index status                                  # show index freshness vs HEAD                                          
  kg-index reindex-file <file_path>                                                                                        
  kg-index extractors                              # list available extractors                                             
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## indexer_README.md                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  - Cold-start vs incremental                                                                                              
  - The supersede pattern (close old via valid_to; insert new)                                                             
  - How to wire into git hooks (post-commit) OR use the watcher daemon                                                     
  - Performance targets (NFR-1 from REQ-INIT-6)                                                                            
  - Troubleshooting: stale index, missing extractor, parse errors                                                          
  - Cross-refs: V2 schema, build/kg/extractors/, db/watcher/, REQ-INIT-6 FR-5                                              
                                                                                                                           
  ## Constraints                                                                                                           
  - Python 3.11+, stdlib + pydantic + PyYAML + lazy tree-sitter                                                            
  - All Postgres via subprocess `psql` (consistent with rest of Spine)                                                     
  - ≤300 indexer, ≤250 parser_runtime, ≤180 diff_engine, ≤120 watcher_extension, ≤120 cli, ≤120 README                     
  - Transactional applies (BEGIN; ...; COMMIT) per file batch                                                              
  - Don't modify V2 schema or extractor configs                                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Edit files outside `build/kg/indexer/`                                                                                 
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Try to actually run against a live repo                                                                                
  - Modify db/watcher/ — just hook into it via watcher_extension.py                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  Report back with all 7 file paths.

❯ Implement the remaining 4 stub KG MCP tools with real Postgres queries: `trace_dependency`, `code_neighborhood`,         
  `doc_for_region`, `who_owns`. Implements `STORY-6.5.3`, `STORY-6.5.4`, `STORY-6.5.6`, `STORY-6.5.7` in                   
  `docs/BACKLOG.md`.                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  Wave 4 shipped real implementations of `find_callers` and `impact_radius` in `shared/mcp/tools/kg.py`. Other KG tools    
  are still stubs. This story replaces them with real impls following the same patterns.                                   
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-6 §6.5 FR-6 (full tool spec for all 4)                                                         
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — EPIC-6.5 stories                                                                                  
  3. `shared/mcp/tools/kg.py` — read CURRENT state; you'll add 4 real impls. The pattern is set by `find_callers` and      
  `impact_radius` (recursive CTEs, subprocess psql, audit hook). MATCH that style.                                         
  4. `db/flyway/sql/V2__spine_kg_schema.sql` — node + edge tables, indexes                                                 
  5. `shared/mcp/schemas/envelopes.py` — `ToolResponse` etc.                                                               
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — modify ONE file                                                                                              
  `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/shared/mcp/tools/kg.py`                                              
                                                                                                                           
  Replace 4 stub functions with real implementations. Leave `find_callers`, `impact_radius`, `graph_query`,                
  `hybrid_search`, `find_by_satisfies` UNCHANGED (other stories).                                                          
                                                                                                                           
  ## trace_dependency (STORY-6.5.3)                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  **Input:**                                                                                                               
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class TraceDependencyInput(BaseModel):                                                                                   
      from_symbol: str        # source symbol                                                                              
      to_symbol: str          # target symbol                                                                              
      repo: str                                                                                                            
      max_depth: int = 5      # cap search depth                                                                           
      edge_types: list[str] = ["CALLS", "IMPORTS"]   # which edges to traverse                                             
      commit_sha: str | None = None                                                                                        
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Output:**                                                                                                              
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class DependencyPath(BaseModel):                                                                                         
      path: list[str]         # node IDs from source → target                                                              
      edges: list[str]        # edge types along the path                                                                  
      depth: int                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                           
  class TraceDependencyOutput(BaseModel):                                                                                  
      status: ToolStatus                                                                                                   
      from_symbol: str                                                                                                     
      to_symbol: str                                                                                                       
      paths_found: list[DependencyPath]   # up to N shortest paths                                                         
      no_path: bool                        # true if no connection within max_depth                                        
      query_latency_ms: int                                                                                                
      audit_id: UUID                                                                                                       
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Implementation:** Resolve both symbols to node IDs; run recursive CTE bidirectional BFS limited by `max_depth` and     
  `edge_types`; return up to 5 shortest paths.                                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## code_neighborhood (STORY-6.5.4)                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  **Input:**                                                                                                               
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class CodeNeighborhoodInput(BaseModel):                                                                                  
      target: str             # symbol OR file path                                                                        
      repo: str                                                                                                            
      radius: int = 2         # N-hop subgraph                                                                             
      edge_types: list[str] | None = None   # None = all edges                                                             
      commit_sha: str | None = None                                                                                        
      limit: int = 200        # max nodes returned                                                                         
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Output:**                                                                                                              
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class NeighborNode(BaseModel):                                                                                           
      node_id: str                                                                                                         
      name: str                                                                                                            
      type: str                                                                                                            
      path: str                                                                                                            
      distance: int            # hops from target                                                                          
                                                                                                                           
  class NeighborEdge(BaseModel):                                                                                           
      from_node_id: str                                                                                                    
      to_node_id: str                                                                                                      
      type: str                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  class CodeNeighborhoodOutput(BaseModel):                                                                                 
      status: ToolStatus                                                                                                   
      target: str                                                                                                          
      nodes: list[NeighborNode]                                                                                            
      edges: list[NeighborEdge]                                                                                            
      query_latency_ms: int                                                                                                
      audit_id: UUID                                                                                                       
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Implementation:** Resolve target to node ID; recursive CTE expanding both directions (incoming + outgoing edges) up to 
   `radius` hops; include edges between returned nodes.                                                                    
                                                                                                                           
  ## doc_for_region (STORY-6.5.6)                                                                                          
                                                                                                                           
  **Input:**                                                                                                               
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class DocForRegionInput(BaseModel):                                                                                      
      file: str               # file path                                                                                  
      line_start: int | None = None                                                                                        
      line_end: int | None = None                                                                                          
      repo: str                                                                                                            
      commit_sha: str | None = None                                                                                        
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Output:**                                                                                                              
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class DocReference(BaseModel):                                                                                           
      doc_type: str           # REQ | ADR | PRD | TRD | Roadmap | MemoryLesson | README                                    
      doc_id: str             # e.g., REQ-INIT-1, ADR-005                                                                  
      title: str                                                                                                           
      relevance: str          # CITES | OWNS | TESTS | TOUCHES | DERIVED_FROM                                              
      path: str               # doc file path                                                                              
      line_in_doc: int | None                                                                                              
                                                                                                                           
  class DocForRegionOutput(BaseModel):                                                                                     
      status: ToolStatus                                                                                                   
      file: str                                                                                                            
      region: str                                                                                                          
      docs: list[DocReference]                                                                                             
      query_latency_ms: int                                                                                                
      audit_id: UUID                                                                                                       
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Implementation:**                                                                                                      
  1. Find code nodes in the file (optionally filter by line range)                                                         
  2. For each code node, find incoming edges of types `CITES`, `OWNS`, `TESTS`, `TOUCHES`, `DERIVED_FROM`, `DECIDED_BY`    
  where the from-node is a Document node                                                                                   
  3. Return deduplicated list ordered by relevance type then proximity                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  ## who_owns (STORY-6.5.7)                                                                                                
                                                                                                                           
  **Input:**                                                                                                               
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class WhoOwnsInput(BaseModel):                                                                                           
      target: str             # file or symbol                                                                             
      repo: str                                                                                                            
      commit_sha: str | None = None                                                                                        
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Output:**                                                                                                              
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class Owner(BaseModel):                                                                                                  
      owner_type: str         # Role | Person | Team | ADR | Memory                                                        
      owner_id: str                                                                                                        
      confidence: float                                                                                                    
      via: str                # OWNED_BY edge | inferred from git blame | from memory lesson                               
                                                                                                                           
  class WhoOwnsOutput(BaseModel):                                                                                          
      status: ToolStatus                                                                                                   
      target: str                                                                                                          
      owners: list[Owner]                                                                                                  
      query_latency_ms: int                                                                                                
      audit_id: UUID                                                                                                       
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Implementation:**                                                                                                      
  1. Resolve target → node ID (file or symbol)                                                                             
  2. Query `OWNED_BY` edges incoming                                                                                       
  3. Fall back to memory lessons: query MemoryLesson nodes with edges pointing at this file/symbol                         
  4. If still empty: returns empty list (don't fabricate owners)                                                           
                                                                                                                           
  ## Style + constraints                                                                                                   
  - Match the existing patterns in kg.py (subprocess psql, point-in-time queries, audit hook, recursive CTEs)              
  - Replace `# TODO STORY-6.5.X: real implementation` comments with actual impls                                           
  - Pydantic v2                                                                                                            
  - ≤+~400 lines total added to kg.py (it grows from ~600 to ~1000 lines)                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## Constraints                                                                                                           
  - All queries support `commit_sha` for point-in-time                                                                     
  - Target latency per spec: code_neighborhood ≤2hops ≤50ms p95; trace_dependency ≤200ms p95; doc_for_region ≤100ms;       
  who_owns ≤50ms                                                                                                           
  - Don't touch `graph_query`, `hybrid_search`, `find_by_satisfies` stubs                                                  
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Edit any file outside `shared/mcp/tools/kg.py`                                                                         
  - Modify the schemas/envelopes.py or server.py                                                                           
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Try to run queries                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                           
  Report back: file path, new line count, the 4 new function signatures.

❯ Wire the Orchestrator's `verify_audit` MCP tool to actually invoke TRON's `AuditManager` (replace stub with real         
  implementation). Implements `STORY-8.5.1` (Orchestrator invokes AuditManager on engineer's output) and `STORY-8.5.2`     
  (Verify writes findings to spine_audit) in `docs/BACKLOG.md`.                                                            
                                                                                                                           
  Spine project root: `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment`.                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Context                                                                                                               
  `shared/mcp/tools/verify.py` has a stub `verify_audit` MCP tool. TRON's `AuditManager` is now at                         
  `verify/tron/agents/manager.py` (post-Phase-1 subtree merge). This story wires the stub to actually call `AuditManager`, 
   map TRON's `FindingOutput` to Spine's `VerifyFindings` envelope, and persist findings to `spine_audit`.                 
                                                                                                                           
  ## Inputs to read first                                                                                                  
  1. `docs/PRD.md` REQ-INIT-8 §8.5 FR-4 (verify_audit spec — full)                                                         
  2. `docs/BACKLOG.md` — INIT-8 EPIC-8.4 (Orchestrator wiring)                                                             
  3. `shared/mcp/tools/verify.py` — current stub                                                                           
  4. `shared/mcp/tools/iso.py` — companion wrapper that already shows the lazy-import + cost-attribution pattern (mirror   
  it)                                                                                                                      
  5. `shared/schemas/build/build_artifact.py` — the `BuildArtifact` input contract                                         
  6. `shared/audit/audit_record.py` — write findings to audit                                                              
  7. `verify/tron/agents/manager.py` (if exists) OR `verify/tron/agents/` — find AuditManager entry point; just READ to    
  understand signature, don't modify                                                                                       
  8. `verify/tron/schemas/verification.py` (if exists) — TRON's `FindingOutput` shape                                      
                                                                                                                           
  ## Output — modify ONE file                                                                                              
  `/Users/khashsarrafi/Projects/Apps/SpineDevelopment/shared/mcp/tools/verify.py`                                          
                                                                                                                           
  Replace the existing `verify_audit` stub with a real implementation. Mirror the pattern from `shared/mcp/tools/iso.py`   
  (lazy TRON import + degraded mode + structured envelope).                                                                
                                                                                                                           
  ## Implementation requirements                                                                                           
                                                                                                                           
  **Real `verify_audit`:**                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                           
  ```python                                                                                                                
  class VerifyAuditInput(BaseModel):                                                                                       
      build_artifact: BuildArtifact      # Pydantic from shared/schemas/build/                                             
      blueprint: Blueprint               # scope: file_patterns, check_types, NOT_IN_SCOPE, iso_agents                     
      project_id: str                                                                                                      
      actor: str = "orchestrator"                                                                                          
      cross_llm_validation: bool = False  # opt-in (STORY-3.7.x)                                                           
      sandbox_layer: bool = True          # run Layer 3 sandbox if available                                               
                                                                                                                           
  class VerifyFindings(BaseModel):                                                                                         
      status: ToolStatus                                                                                                   
      pass_fail: Literal["pass", "fail", "needs_user_review"]                                                              
      findings: list[Finding]            # mirror TRON's FindingOutput shape                                               
      layers_run: list[str]              # which of TRON's 7 layers actually ran                                           
      sandbox_executed: bool                                                                                               
      cross_llm_consensus: bool          # true if multiple providers agreed                                               
      calibration_band: str | None       # from Platt scaling if available                                                 
      duration_ms: int                                                                                                     
      cost_usd: Decimal                                                                                                    
      audit_id: UUID                                                                                                       
      error: ToolError | None                                                                                              
  ```                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                           
  **Implementation steps:**                                                                                                
  1. Validate input: BuildArtifact must be sealed (status="sealed"); blueprint required                                    
  2. Lazy import: `from verify.tron.agents.manager import AuditManager` wrapped in try/except →                            
  `ToolError(code="tron_not_available")` if fails                                                                          
  3. Probe Docker once at module load — if unavailable, set `sandbox_layer=False` regardless of input (degraded mode), log 
   warning                                                                                                                 
  4. Construct TRON's expected AuditRequest shape from `BuildArtifact` + `blueprint`:                                      
     - Files to audit: from `build_artifact.code_changes` paths                                                            
     - Diff hashes: from `build_artifact.code_changes[*].diff_hash`                                                        
     - Scope: blueprint file_patterns + check_types                                                                        
     - Cost cap: from project budget (look up via `shared/cost/router.py`)                                                 
  5. Call `AuditManager.run(audit_request)` — TRON does the heavy lifting (deterministic scanners → ISO swarm →            
  cross-validation → sandbox → calibration)                                                                                
  6. Map TRON's response to `VerifyFindings`:                                                                              
     - Each FindingOutput → Spine Finding (severity, file:line, rule, message, fix_hint, confidence, layers_vouched)       
     - layers_run from TRON's audit metadata                                                                               
     - cross_llm_consensus from TRON's cross-validation result                                                             
     - calibration_band from TRON's Platt scaling output (if active)                                                       
  7. Compute total cost from TRON's per-finding cost rollup                                                                
  8. Persist findings to `spine_audit` via `AuditRecord` (one row per finding for queryability + one summary row for the   
  audit invocation)                                                                                                        
  9. Determine pass_fail:                                                                                                  
     - pass = zero critical findings AND zero high findings                                                                
     - fail = ≥1 critical finding                                                                                          
     - needs_user_review = ≥1 high finding (no criticals)                                                                  
  10. Return `VerifyFindings`                                                                                              
                                                                                                                           
  **Adapter stubs allowed** (clearly marked TODOs) if TRON's exact signatures aren't obvious from a quick scan:            
  - `TODO(STORY-8.5.1): wire to AuditManager.run() actual signature`                                                       
  - Stub returns structurally-correct envelope so callers can integrate today                                              
                                                                                                                           
  **Cost attribution:**                                                                                                    
  - Verify-phase costs land in `spine_recording.costs` with `subsystem="verify"` (per V16 schema)                          
  - Calibration / cross-LLM costs broken out in audit metadata                                                             
                                                                                                                           
  ## Other tool stubs to keep                                                                                              
                                                                                                                           
  Don't touch other stubs in verify.py if they exist (only modify `verify_audit`).                                         
                                                                                                                           
  ## Style                                                                                                                 
  - Match iso.py + sandbox.py patterns (lazy import, degraded mode, structured envelope, TODOs marked clearly)             
  - Pydantic v2                                                                                                            
  - Standard library + pydantic + lazy verify.tron import only                                                             
  - ≤+~200 lines added to verify.py                                                                                        
                                                                                                                           
  ## DO NOT                                                                                                                
  - Modify any TRON code under `verify/`                                                                                   
  - Edit any file outside `shared/mcp/tools/verify.py`                                                                     
  - Spawn other agents                                                                                                     
  - Try to actually run AuditManager                                                                                       
                                                                                                                           
  Report back: file path, new line count, summary of the implementation.
